D2 Vision L1 Remember Atom

Pupillary Light Reflex

slide the ambient luminance and watch the pupil constrict in bright light and dilate in the dark

The iris muscles resize the pupil to regulate retinal illumination — a small aperture in bright light, a wide one in the dark.

Slide the ambient luminance from a moonless night to direct sunlight. Watch the iris close the pupil as light grows and reopen it as the scene darkens, and read the diameter below.
